title: cyperus cyperoides subsp. flavus
intro: Cyperus cyperoides subsp. flavus, also known as the yellow papyrus sedge, is a subspecies of the Cyperus cyperoides plant, which is a type of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. This plant is native to tropical Africa and is widely used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The yellow papyrus sedge has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as to alleviate digestive issues and skin problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to make poultices and salves for wound healing and skin care.
